title: Library Construction Protocol
author: Dakota Betz
materials: Proceed immediately to Adapter Ligation (next section).
Carefully remove and discard the supernatant.
Carefully remove and discard the ethanol.

The double-sided size selection protocol outlined in this appendix is designed for the selection of library molecules (inclusive of adapter) in the range of 250 – 450 bp. To obtain a population of shorter or longer molecules, the protocol may be modified as follows: A B C D 1 Upper size limit Modification Lower size limit Modification 2 Increase Decrease the ratio of the first cut Increase Decrease the ratio of the second cut* 3 Decrease Increase the ratio of the first cut Decrease Increase the ratio of the second cut* * The second size cut should be performed with at least 0.2 volumes of KAPA cleanup beads reagent. Please note that the volume of KAPA cleanup beads needed for the second cut is calculated relative to the volume of the DNA at the start of the size selection procedure, not the volume of the DNA-containing supernatant transferred after the first cut. DNA recovery is dramatically reduced if the difference between first and second cuts is less than ~0.2 volumes. To increase the amount of DNA recovered, >0.2 volumes of KAPA cleanup beads may be used for the second cut, but note that this may result in the recovery of smaller library fragments and/or a broader size distribution. For more information on double-sided size selection, please refer to the KAPA NGS Library Preparation Technical Guide (available on request from Technical Support at sequencing.roche.com/support).

steps.title: for a detailed DNA cleanup protocol
steps.instruction: for a detailed DNA cleanup protocol. Alternatively, prepare a sufficient volume of appropriately diluted Conditioning Solution ( 5 µL per DNA sample, plus excess). Refer to Table 2 (p.

steps.title: for guidelines on the dilution of the Conditioning Solution
steps.instruction: for guidelines on the dilution of the Conditioning Solution. Dilute the amount of dsDNA to be used for library construction as follows: If the DNA preparation does not contain EDTA, dilute in 10 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl (pH 8.0 – 8.

steps.title: in a total of 35 µL...
steps.instruction: in a total of 35 µL If the DNA preparation does contain EDTA, dilute in the EDTA-containing buffer in which samples are currently suspended, in a total of 30 µL . To each reaction with 30 µL of EDTA-containing DNA, add 5 µL of diluted Conditioning Solution.

steps.title: Mix by gentle vortexing or pipetting up and down
steps.instruction: Mix by gentle vortexing or pipetting up and down.

steps.title: Assemble each fragmentation reaction on ice...
steps.instruction: Assemble each fragmentation reaction on ice by adding the components in this order: A B 1 Component Volume 2 Double-stranded DNA (with Conditioning Solution, if needed) 35 μL 3 KAPA Frag Buffer (10X)* 5 μL 4 KAPA Frag Enzyme* 10 μL 5 Total volume: 50 μL * The KAPA Frag Buffer and Enzyme may be pre-mixed and kept on ice prior to reaction setup, and dispensed as a single solution. Please note the volume of buffer is less than the volume of enzyme in this reaction

steps.title: Vortex gently and spin down briefly
steps.instruction: Vortex gently and spin down briefly. Return the plate/tube(s) to ice. Proceed immediately to the next step.

steps.title: Incubate in a thermocycler, pre-cooled to...
steps.instruction: Incubate in a thermocycler, pre-cooled to 4 °C and programmed as outlined below. A heated lid is not required for this step. If used, set the temperature of the heated lid to ≤ 50 °C . A B C 1 Step Temp Time Step Temp Time Step Temp Time 2 Pre-cool block 4°C N/A Pre-cool block 4°C N/A Pre-cool block 4°C N/A 3 Fragmentation 37°C See table below Fragmentation 37°C See table below Fragmentation 37°C See table below 4 HOLD 4°C ∞ HOLD 4°C ∞ HOLD 4°C ∞

steps.title: Transfer reactions to ice, and proceed...
steps.instruction: Transfer reactions to ice, and proceed immediately to End Repair and A-tailing (next section).

steps.title: Two End Repair & A-Tailing Enzyme...
steps.instruction: Two End Repair & A-Tailing Enzyme Mixes are provided for the End Repair and A-tailing step —see Important Parameters for further guidelines.

steps.title: In the same plate/tube(s) in which...
steps.instruction: In the same plate/tube(s) in which enzymatic fragmentation was performed, assemble each End Repair and A-tailing reaction as follows: A B 1 Component Volume 2 Fragmented, double-stranded DNA 50 μL 3 End Repair & A-Tailing Buffer* 7 μL 4 HyperPrep/HyperPlus ERAT Enzyme Mix** 3 μL 5 Total volume: 60 μL * The buffer and enzyme mix should preferably be pre-mixed and added in a single pipetting step. Premixes are stable for ≤24 hrs at room temperature, for ≤3 days at 2°C to 8°C, and for ≤4 weeks at -15°C to -25°C. **Use either the HyperPrep ERAT Enzyme Mix (existing chemisty) or the HyperPlus ERAT Enzyme Mix (enhanced chemistry)

steps.title: Vortex gently and spin down briefly
steps.instruction: Vortex gently and spin down briefly. Return the reaction plate/tube(s) to ice. Proceed immediately to the next step.

steps.title: Incubate in a thermocycler programmed as outlined below
steps.instruction: Incubate in a thermocycler programmed as outlined below. A heated lid is required for this step. If possible, set the temperature of the heated lid to ~ 85 °C (instead of the usual 105 °C ). A B C 1 Step Temp Time 2 End repair and A-tailing 65°C* 30 min 3 HOLD 4°C** ∞ *Both the fragmentation and end repair enzymes are inactivated at 65°C. When reactions are set up according to recommendations, additional fragmentation should be negligible. The brief period of end repair is sufficient for enzymatically fragmented DNA. ** If proceeding to the adapter ligation reaction setup without any delay, the reaction may be cooled to 20°C instead of 4°C.

steps.title: Dilute adapter stocks to the appropriate...
steps.instruction: Dilute adapter stocks to the appropriate concentration, as outlined in Table 4 (see below). Table 4. Recommended adapter concentrations for libraries constructed from 1 ng – 1 μg input DNA* A B C D E F 1 Input DNA Adapter stock concentration Adapter:insert molar ratio Input DNA Adapter stockconcentration Adapter:insert molar ratio 2 1 μg 15 μM 10:1 25 ng 7.5 μM 200:1 3 500 ng 15 μM 20:1 10 ng 3 μM 200:1 4 250 ng 15 μM 40:1 5 ng 1.5 μM 200:1 5 100 ng 15 μM 100:1 2.5 ng 750 nM 200:1 6 50 ng 15 μM 200:1 1 ng 300 nM 200:1 * Adapter:insert molar ratio calculations are based on a mode DNA fragment length of 200 bp, and will be higher for longer DNA fragments, or slightly lower for DNA fragmented to a mode size <200 bp. The lower adapter:insert molar ratios recommended for inputs >100 ng represent a fair compromise between library construction efficiency and cost; higher library yields will be achieved if a higher adapter concentration is used.

steps.title: In the same plate/tube(s) in which...
steps.instruction: In the same plate/tube(s) in which end repair and A-tailing was performed, assemble each adapter ligation reaction as follows: A B 1 Component Volume 2 End repair and A-tailing reaction product 60 μL 3 Adapter stock (diluted as per Table 4 on p. 5) 5 μL 4 PCR-grade water* 5 μL 5 Ligation Buffer* 30 μL 6 DNA Ligase* 10 μL 7 Total volume: 110 μL * The water, buffer and ligase enzyme should preferably be premixed and added in a single pipetting step. Premixes are stable for ≤24 hrs at room temperature, for ≤3 days at 2°C to 8°C, and for ≤4 weeks at -15°C to -25°C.

steps.title: Mix thoroughly and centrifuge briefly
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly and centrifuge briefly.

steps.title: Incubate at 20 °C for 00:15:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 20 °C for 00:15:00 . Note: to achieve higher conversion rates and library yields, particularly for low-input samples, consider increasing the ligation time to a maximum of 04:00:00 at 20 °C or Overnight at 2 °C to 8 °C . Please note that longer ligation times may lead to increased levels of adapter-dimer. Adapter concentrations may have to be optimized if ligation times are extended significantly.

steps.title: In the same plate/tube(s), perform a 0
steps.instruction: In the same plate/tube(s), perform a 0.8X beadbased cleanup by combining the following: A B 1 Component Volume 2 Adapter ligation reaction product 110 μL 3 KAPA cleanup beads 88 μL 4 Total volume: 198 μL

steps.title: Mix thoroughly by vortexing and/or pipetting...
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly by vortexing and/or pipetting up and down multiple times.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature for 00:05:00 – 00:15:00 to bind DNA to the beads.

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads. Incubate until the liquid is clear.

steps.title: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet,...
steps.instruction: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet, add 200 µL of 80% ethanol.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet at room temperature for ≥ 00:00:30 .

steps.title: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet,...
steps.instruction: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet, add 200 µL of 80% ethanol.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet at room temperature for ≥ 00:00:30 .

steps.title: Carefully remove and discard the ethanol
steps.instruction: Carefully remove and discard the ethanol. Try to remove all residual ethanol without disturbing the beads.

steps.title: Dry the beads at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Dry the beads at room temperature for 00:03:00 – 00:05:00 , or until all of the ethanol has evaporated. Caution: over-drying the beads may result in reduced yield.

steps.title: Remove the plate/tube(s) from the magnet
steps.instruction: Remove the plate/tube(s) from the magnet.

steps.title: Resuspend the beads
steps.instruction: Resuspend the beads: • in 25 µL of elution buffer ( 10 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl, pH 8.0 – 8.5) to proceed with Library Amplification (step 5), or • in 55 µL of elution buffer ( 10 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl, pH 8.0 – 8.5) to proceed with double-sided size selection (Appendix 1).

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature for 00:02:00 to elute DNA off the beads.

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads. Incubate until the liquid is clear.

steps.title: Transfer the clear supernatant to a new plate/tube(s)
steps.instruction: Transfer the clear supernatant to a new plate/tube(s): • to proceed with Library Amplification (step 5), transfer 20 µL of supernatant, or • to proceed with double-sided size selection (Appendix 1), transfer 50 µL of supernatant.

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: Please refer to Important Parameters: Library Amplification (p. 7) and the KAPA NGS Library Preparation Technical Guide (available on request from Technical Support at sequencing.roche.com/support) for more information on optimizing library amplification.

steps.title: Assemble each library amplification reaction as follows
steps.instruction: Assemble each library amplification reaction as follows: A B 1 Component Volume 2 KAPA HiFi HotStart ReadyMix (2X) 25 μL 3 Library Amplification Primer Mix (10X)* 5 μL 4 Adapter-ligated library 20 μL 5 Total volume: 50 μL * Or other, suitable 10X library amplification primer mix.The recommended final concentration of each primer in the library amplification reaction is 0.5 – 4 μM. Also refer to Important Parameters: Library Amplification (p. 7).

steps.title: Mix thoroughly and centrifuge briefly
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly and centrifuge briefly.

steps.title: Amplify using the following cycling protocol
steps.instruction: Amplify using the following cycling protocol: A B C D 1 Step Temp Duration Cycle 2 Initial denaturation 98°C 45 sec 1 3 Denaturation 98°C 15 sec Minimum numberrequired for optimalamplification (Table 5 or 6) 4 Annealing* 60°C 30 sec 5 Extension 72°C 30 sec 6 Finalextension 72°C 1 min 7 HOLD 4°C ∞ 1 8 1 * Optimization of the annealing temperature may be required for nonstandard (i.e., other than Illumina TruSeq) adapter/primer combinations.

steps.title: In the library amplification plate/tube(s), perform...
steps.instruction: In the library amplification plate/tube(s), perform a 1X bead-based cleanup by combining the following: A B 1 Component Volume 2 Library amplification reaction product 50 μL 3 KAPA cleanup beads 50 μL 4 Total volume: 100 μL

steps.title: Mix thoroughly by vortexing and/or pipetting...
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly by vortexing and/or pipetting up and down multiple times.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature for 00:05:00 – 00:15:00 to bind DNA to the beads.

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads. Incubate until the liquid is clear.

steps.title: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet,...
steps.instruction: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet, add 200 µL of 80% ethanol.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet at room temperature for ≥ 00:00:30 .

steps.title: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet,...
steps.instruction: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet, add 200 µL of 80% ethanol.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet at room temperature for ≥ 00:00:30 .

steps.title: Carefully remove and discard the ethanol
steps.instruction: Carefully remove and discard the ethanol. Try to remove all residual ethanol without disturbing the beads.

steps.title: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet,...
steps.instruction: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet, add 200 µL of 80% ethanol.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet at room temperature for ≥ 00:00:30 .

steps.title: Carefully remove and discard the ethanol
steps.instruction: Carefully remove and discard the ethanol. Try to remove all residual ethanol without disturbing the beads.

steps.title: Dry the beads at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Dry the beads at room temperature for 00:03:00 – 00:05:00 , or until all of the ethanol has evaporated. Caution: over-drying the beads may result in reduced yield.

steps.title: Remove the plate/tube(s) from the magnet
steps.instruction: Remove the plate/tube(s) from the magnet.

steps.title: Thoroughly resuspend the beads in an...
steps.instruction: Thoroughly resuspend the beads in an appropriate volume of elution buffer ( 10 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl, pH 8.0 – 8.5) or PCR-grade water. Note: If proceeding with a second post amplification cleanup, or double-sided size selection (Appendix 1), resuspend the beads in 55 µL of elution buffer.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature for 00:02:00 to elute DNA off the beads.

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads. Incubate until the liquid is clear.

steps.title: Transfer the clear supernatant to a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the clear supernatant to a new plate/tube(s) and proceed with size selection (refer to Appendix 1), library QC, target capture or sequencing, as appropriate. Store purified, amplified libraries at 2 °C to 8 °C for 1 – 2 weeks, or at -15 °C to -25 °C .

steps.title: Perform the first (0
steps.instruction: Perform the first (0.7X) size cut (to exclude library molecules larger than ~450 bp) by combining the following: A B 1 Component Volume 2 DNA to be size selected 50 μL 3 KAPA cleanup beads 35 μL 4 Total volume per well/tube: 85 μL

steps.title: Mix thoroughly by vortexing and/or pipetting...
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly by vortexing and/or pipetting up and down multiple times.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature for 00:05:00 – 00:15:00 in to bind library molecules larger than ~450 bp to the beads.

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads. Incubate until the liquid is clear.

steps.title: Carefully transfer ~ 80 µL of...
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer ~ 80 µL of supernatant containing library molecules smaller than ~450 bp to a new plate/tube. It is critical that no beads are transferred with the supernatant. Discard the plate/tube(s) with the beads to which library molecules larger than ~450 bp were bound.

steps.title: Perform the second size cut (0
steps.instruction: Perform the second size cut (0.9X), to retain library molecules >250 bp) by combining the following: A B 1 Component Volume 2 Supernatant from first size cut 80 μL 3 KAPA cleanup beads 10 μL 4 Total volume per well/tube: 90 μL

steps.title: Mix thoroughly by vortexing and/or pipetting...
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly by vortexing and/or pipetting up and down multiple times.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature for 00:05:00 – 00:15:00 n to bind library molecules larger than ~250 bp to the beads.

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads. Incubate until the liquid is clear.

steps.title: Carefully remove and discard the supernatant,...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove and discard the supernatant, which contains library molecules smaller than ~250 bp.

steps.title: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet,...
steps.instruction: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet, add 200 µL of 80% ethanol.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet at room temperature for ≥ 00:00:30 .

steps.title: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet,...
steps.instruction: Keeping the plate/tube(s) on the magnet, add 200 µL of 80% ethanol.

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) on the magnet at room temperature for ≥ 00:00:30 .

steps.title: Carefully remove and discard the ethanol
steps.instruction: Carefully remove and discard the ethanol. Try to remove all residual ethanol without disturbing the beads.

steps.title: Dry the beads for 00:03:00 –...
steps.instruction: Dry the beads for 00:03:00 – 00:05:00 at Room temperature , or until all of the ethanol has evaporated. Caution: over-drying the beads may result in reduced yield.

steps.title: Remove the plate/tube(s) from the magnet
steps.instruction: Remove the plate/tube(s) from the magnet.

steps.title: Thoroughly resuspend the beads in the...
steps.instruction: Thoroughly resuspend the beads in the required volume of elution buffer ( 10 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl, pH 8.0 – 8.5).

steps.title: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate/tube(s) at room temperature for 00:02:00 to elute DNA off the beads.

steps.title: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads
steps.instruction: Place the plate/tube(s) on a magnet to capture the beads. Incubate until the liquid is clear.

steps.title: Transfer the clear supernatant with size-selected...
steps.instruction: Transfer the clear supernatant with size-selected DNA to a new plate/tube(s) and proceed with the next step in your workflow, or store DNA at 2 °C to 8 °C for 1 – 2 weeks, or at -15 °C to -25 °C .

steps.title: Fragmentation guidelines provided in the Library...
steps.instruction: Fragmentation guidelines provided in the Library Construction Protocol: Enzymatic Fragmentation (step 1) may not result in the optimal library size distribution for your specific DNA samples. For this reason, precious samples should not be used when evaluating the KAPA HyperPlus Kit for the first time. Instead, fragmentation parameters should be optimized in the context of the KAPA HyperPlus workflow, using a non-precious, bulk DNA sample that is representative of the actual samples to be processed. The information in this Appendix should be considered during the experimental design for your evaluation of the KAPA HyperPlus Kit.

steps.title: Quantification of Input DNA The Qubit...
steps.instruction: Quantification of Input DNA The Qubit fluorometer is recommended for the quantification of high-quality DNA, whereas the KAPA hgDNA Quantification and QC Kit provides both concentration and quality information for FFPE DNA.

steps.title: Handling of DNA Samples Containing EDTA...
steps.instruction: Handling of DNA Samples Containing EDTA If the DNA samples contain EDTA, perform a 3X beadbased cleanup with KAPA cleanup beads to remove EDTA prior to fragmentation. Please refer to the relevant Technical Data Sheet (KR1705 or KR1245) for a detailed DNA cleanup protocol.

steps.title: For example
steps.instruction: For example: • If your DNA samples are in TE buffer, and your input into library construction is 100 ng , dilute 100 ng of each sample into a final volume of 30 µL (i.e., to 3.33 ng/μL ) using TE buffer. • All samples will now contain the same final EDTA concentration once diluted to 50 µL for fragmentation. This concentration is: EDTA concentration in TE buffer x (30 μL/50 μL) = 1 mM x (30 μL/50 μL) = 0.6 millimolar (mM) • Make a 2.2-fold dilution of the Conditioning Solution (as per Table 3 on p. 4), and follow the Library Construction Protocol (second half of step 1).

steps.title: If you are unsure about the...
steps.instruction: If you are unsure about the presence or concentration of EDTA in your DNA samples, remove the EDTA by performing a column- or bead-based purification or buffer exchange prior to enzymatic fragmentation, or follow the procedure outlined below:

steps.title: Set up a series of test...
steps.instruction: Set up a series of test reactions with the appropriate amount of input DNA, and different final concentrations of Conditioning Solution.

steps.title: Include at least one reaction with...
steps.instruction: Include at least one reaction with the same input of control DNA known to be EDTA-free. The control DNA should preferably be of the same type and quality as the test samples.

steps.title: Fragment the DNA using the appropriate...
steps.instruction: Fragment the DNA using the appropriate parameters, as outlined in the Library Construction Protocol: Enzymatic Fragmentation (step 1). Complete the library construction process, and compare library size distributions for the test and control samples using an electrophoretic system (see Important Parameters: Evaluating the Success of Library Construction, p. 8).

steps.title: Titrate the final concentration of Conditioning...
steps.instruction: Titrate the final concentration of Conditioning Solution in the reaction until the test samples yield similar fragmentation profiles as the EDTA-free control sample, or until the desired library size distribution has been achieved.

steps.title: A two-step strategy may be the best
steps.instruction: A two-step strategy may be the best. Start with 3 – 4 test samples covering a broad range of final Conditioning Solution concentrations, then perform a finer titration over a narrower concentration range.
